title: define the term 'construction' commonly applied to a building contract

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: define the term 'construction' commonly applied to a building contract

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Construction refers to the process of building or erecting a structure, typically a building. It involves the planning, design, engineering, and execution of a project, including excavating and preparing the site, pouring foundations, assembling the structure, installing utilities and mechanical systems, and finishing the interior and exterior. Construction is commonly applied to a building contract as a legal agreement between a property owner (employer) and a contractor (employee) that outlines the terms and conditions of the construction project, including the scope, budget, timeline, and payment schedule.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: define the terms ‘building’ and 'party' commonly applied to a building contract

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Building: It refers to a structure that is either being constructed or already built and that is intended for use as a dwelling, office, or other purpose. A building can be a single-family home, a commercial complex, an industrial plant, or any other type of structure.

Party: In a building contract, 'party' is a legal term used to refer to the individuals or entities involved in the contract. There are two primary parties to a building contract: the client (sometimes referred to as the employer) and the contractor. The client is the person or organization who is commissioning the building work and who will ultimately own the finished product. The contractor is the person or organization who is responsible for carrying out the building work. Other parties may be involved in the contract, such as subcontractors, suppliers, architects, engineers, and consultants, who may be referred to as third-party contractors.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: A contract sum is the total amount of money agreed upon between the client (employer) and the contractor in a building contract. It is the financial value of the agreement in which the parties agree to perform all of the work and supply all of the materials necessary to complete the construction project. The contract sum typically includes all costs associated with the project such as labor, materials, equipment, permits, and fees. It is an essential component of a construction contract as it establishes the fixed price at which the work will be completed, and both parties are bound by the contract sum unless there are agreed-upon additions or deductions for changes or modifications.

evidence.snippet: define the terms ‘building’ and 'party' commonly applied to a building contract
